prompt: Design a Offline Lease & Service Operations Portal to run an on-premise program office that manages facility leases while tracking physical items through intake-to-delivery service workflows. Users include Students (request services and track their own items), Faculty Advisors (review exceptions and oversee assigned cohorts), Corporate Mentors (view progress and approve appeals for their internship cohort), and Department Admins (configure catalogs, contracts, and access rules). The React web interface provides a searchable service catalog with category and tag filters, keyword search, price and duration sorting, favorites, and browsing history stored on the device so results remain available offline. For each incoming pair of shoes, staff create a shoe profile with brand, material, color, size, defect notes, and up to 8 intake photos (JPEG/PNG, max 5 MB each), then print and attach a unique serial plus barcode label; every handoff step requires a barcode scan to log custody events and allows instant lookup of historical service records. Lease contracts are managed end-to-end with guided screens for creation, renewal, amendment, and termination, including configurable billing rules such as fixed rent due on the 1st, tiered rent with breakpoints, or revenue-share with a monthly true-up; deposits and security deposits support partial refunds and must be reconciled within 10 business days after termination. The system shows contract expirations in a dashboard 90/30/7 days prior and supports offline shipping orders for completed items using saved US addresses, proof-of-delivery capture via signature photo, and exception sign-off when deliveries fail. An exception and appeal loop flags missed check-ins, misidentification, or suspected buddy punching, then lets the impacted user submit an appeal with evidence attachments and receive an on-screen decision and audit trail. The backend uses Express to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the React client over the local network, keeping the UI decoupled from data and enabling role-based menus and API authorization. MongoDB persists contracts, lease rule versions, shoe profiles, scan events, service catalog entries, shipping orders, exceptions, appeals, and member tags. RBAC enforces both feature access and data scope by school, major, class, and internship cohort; for example, a Corporate Mentor can only view records for their assigned cohort, while Department Admins can administer catalogs and contracts across all scopes. Identity runs fully offline: users sign up and sign in with username and password only; passwords must be at least 12 characters with a stored salted hash, and password resets require answering a pre-registered local security question (no email/SMS). Sessions support multi-device login with a 30-minute idle timeout and an admin-driven forced logout for compromised devices. Member tagging supports static tags and rule-based computed tags (for example, High-Risk Exceptions if 3 anomalies occur within 14 days), with scheduled recomputation every night at 2:00 AM server time, import/export via local CSV files, versioning for rule changes, and a traceable tag-change history. Attachments are stored on local disk with file-type validation and SHA-256 fingerprints; sensitive fields (addresses, deposit amounts, and identity metadata) are encrypted at rest, and audit logs are append-only with retention of 7 years to support internal compliance without relying on any external services.
